facts.text: Willis Reed limped out of the tunnel for Game 7 of the 1970 Finals, hit his first two shots, and the Garden has never, ever been louder.
While everyone remembers Willis Reed's tunnel walk, Walt 'Clyde' Frazier quietly dropped 36 points and 19 assists in that same Game 7. Clyde never lets anyone forget it. Correctly.
The Knicks won it all in 1970, 1973... and then made everyone wait 53 years. Character development.
On June 13, 2026, Jalen Brunson scored 45 points in Game 5 in San Antonio, a Knicks Finals record, tying Michael Jordan for the most points ever in a road Finals-clinching game.
The 2025-26 Knicks are the first team in NBA history to win the NBA Cup AND the NBA championship in the same season. The double. Nobody else has one.
OG Anunoby's tip-in with 2.1 seconds left won Game 4 of the 2026 Finals, 107-106. Mike Brown said he didn't know if there was a bigger play in the history of Knicks basketball.
The Knicks came back from 29 points down in Game 4 of the 2026 Finals. Twenty. Nine. The Spurs are still in that timeout.
The Knicks trailed by double digits in ALL FOUR of their 2026 Finals wins. Cardiologists in the tri-state area reported record business.
Brunson, Bridges, and Hart, the 'Nova Knicks', are the first trio of teammates ever to win both an NCAA title and an NBA championship together.
In January 2026 the Knicks lost 9 of 11 games and the city wanted everyone fired. They then went 28-11 and won the whole thing. Never trust a Knicks fan in January.
After the Hawks went up 2-1 in the first round, the 2026 Knicks ripped off a 13-game playoff win streak and won 15 of their last 16 playoff games, including nine on the road.
Mike Brown was fired by the Kings 18 months before coaching the Knicks to a championship. Sacramento fans, we owe you one. Just one though.
Knicks-Spurs 2026 was the most-watched NBA Finals since 1998, averaging over 20 million viewers. New York makes everything must-see TV.
Jalen Brunson was the 33rd pick in the 2018 draft. Thirty-two teams' scouting departments would like this fact removed from the app.
John Starks' left-handed dunk over the Bulls in the '93 playoffs, 'The Dunk', remains the most replayed two points in Garden history.
Larry Johnson's four-point play against the Pacers in the '99 East finals is one of the loudest moments ever recorded indoors. Grandmama delivered.
The 1999 Knicks are still the only 8-seed to ever reach the NBA Finals. Chaos is a Knicks tradition.
Reggie Miller scored 8 points in 9 seconds at the Garden in 1995 while staring at Spike Lee. We do not discuss this fact. It is included for historical completeness only.
Linsanity, February 2012: an undrafted guard sleeping on his teammate's couch dropped 38 on the Lakers. For two weeks the Garden was the center of the universe.
Patrick Ewing played 15 seasons of Hall of Fame basketball in New York and never got his ring. The 2026 banner is partly his. That's canon now.
Red Holzman coached both Knicks titles ('70, '73) preaching two rules: 'hit the open man' and 'see the ball.' Mike Brown's 'possession over outcome' is the same religion, new sermon.
Spike Lee has held Knicks season tickets since 1985 and has spent an estimated eight figures on them. He was in the building in San Antonio when the drought ended. Obviously.
Celebrity Row regulars include Spike Lee, Timothée Chalamet, Ben Stiller, Tracy Morgan, Chris Rock, and Fat Joe. The Garden is the only arena where the crowd has its own IMDb page.
'Bing Bong' started as a fan interview soundbite in 2021 and became the unofficial city anthem within 48 hours. This app's main button is a tribute.
Dancing Larry has been sprinting around section 106 to 'Shout' during fourth quarters for decades. He has never missed. MVP consideration is warranted.
The 'DE-FENSE' chant? Invented at Madison Square Garden in the 1960s-70s Knicks era. Every other arena is doing a cover version.
Knicks fans sold out the Garden for lottery teams. For LOTTERY teams. Seventeen straight years of sellouts through the worst of it. That's not fandom, that's a covenant.
Walt 'Clyde' Frazier has described Knicks basketball as 'dishing and swishing, posting and toasting, wheeling and dealing' for 30+ years on MSG Network and has never once repeated himself.
The Garden is called 'The Mecca of Basketball' and sits on top of a working train station. Even our arena has a commute.
Madison Square Garden is the oldest arena in the NBA and somehow still the loudest. Age is a number, decibels are forever.
In 2026, Mitchell Robinson grabbed 10 rebounds in the Game 5 clincher, including a save-the-season offensive board off a missed free throw. Unnoticed by the world. Never by us.
Karl-Anthony Towns shot 45.6% from three in the 2026 playoffs as a seven-footer. That is not a normal sentence.
Brunson in the clutch during 2025-26: 57.1% from three when it mattered most. The nickname 'Captain Clutch' was not a marketing decision. It was a diagnosis.
The Knicks' 2026 title run included a 22-point fourth-quarter comeback against Cleveland in the East finals. The fourth quarter is simply when the Knicks decided games start.
Jalen Brunson cried with his dad, Knicks assistant Rick Brunson, after Game 5. His dad was represented as a player by Leon Rose, the executive who signed Jalen. Full-circle doesn't begin to cover it.
